configuration.loadGenerator: one pinned k6 pod per accepted measurement stage (base, extension batch 1, and extension batch 2); no per-cell pod churn
configuration.transportStorage: node-local hostPath; excluded from measured application data
configuration.databaseStorage: memory-backed emptyDir with a 512Mi limit; isolates benchmark data from the etcd disk

repeatabilityExtension.rule: For every stack/profile group that failed only a coefficient-of-variation threshold after the complete three-repetition matrix, retain all base samples and add batches of three repetitions for every flagged group. Stop after the first batch that passes every original threshold, or after two batches. Do not replace or discard any sample.

rejectedStageAttempts.gate: continuous explicit control-plane and node health plus the post-measure API gate
rejectedStageAttempts.observation: a transient control-plane readiness loss made both explicit endpoints and homelab-01 unavailable to the monitor; the immediate post-measure gate still found endpoint 2 unavailable
rejectedStageAttempts.resolution: quarantined the whole stage and reran all nine declared extension cells after a new 24-sample stability gate

admissionRetries.observation: the configured Kubernetes client briefly missed readiness while both explicit control-plane endpoints and both nodes remained healthy
admissionRetries.resolution: aligned the extension admission gate with the campaign rule: explicit endpoints and nodes are authoritative, while a configured-client-only miss is retained as a warning

evidenceTransfer.observation: the kubectl tar stream reported SIGPIPE after the complete result payload had already been extracted
evidenceTransfer.resolution: validated every staged count and JSON document before the results-only merge

publicationQualification.rule: A campaign may be published as qualified, never accepted, when every remaining failure is a repeatability-CV miss on a non-Nidus peer after that group reaches the predeclared extension limit; all Nidus candidate/control groups, correctness checks, provenance checks, and infrastructure gates must pass. The exact peer failure remains visible in the summary and website.

included: execution order and database row-count integrity checks
cluster admission, continuous health, API gates, and idle CPU samples
candidate, control, peer, infrastructure, node, pod, and pinned k6 runner identities
base-analysis decision and both adaptive repeatability-extension completion records
excluded: peer application source and SQL setup scripts
application logs, unrelated homelab workload snapshots, and private network addresses
